headline: Anthropic's Mythos model family, led by Fable 5, dominated the quarter while established agent techniques cooled.
body: Anthropic released the Mythos family with Fable 5 (Mythos 5 with guardrails) made generally available at no extra cost across Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22, with strong benchmark claims and a string of demonstrations: autonomous Factorio play, a physics-derived solar system simulation predicting eclipses, a synchronized fluid simulation set to a Beethoven remix, and a 3D-printable model built inside a Claude-built CAD editor. Meanwhile a cluster of agent and workflow techniques weakened in rotation, including browser automation, prompt engineering, multi-session workflows, context management, model evaluation, and recursive self-improvement. New entrants broke out around infrastructure and parallelism, with throughput scaling, CPU offloading, and Untied Ulysses surfacing alongside view synthesis. RAG was reframed as a broader retrieval paradigm rather than a dead end, citing Cursor semantic search gains of 24% answer accuracy.

cited_items.title: Claude Fable + Mythos 5 Are Live & The Numbers are INSANE
